title: micromeria lepida subsp. bolleana
intro: Micromeria lepida subsp. bolleana, also known as Bolleana mountain tea or Sardinian mountain tea, is a subspecies of the Micromeria lepida plant, a type of flowering plant in the mint family. It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region, particularly in Sardinia, Italy. The plant is rich in flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess medicinal properties that may help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Micromeria lepida subsp. bolleana is often used to make teas and infusions that can help soothe the respiratory system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
